name: wargame3
scenario_label: Wargame 3 — Brega Amphibious Assault (173-unit OOB, 17 phases)
purpose_en: Wargame 3 models a contested amphibious assault in the Brega coastal region. Red Force aims to seize the Nasser-Brega pipeline midpoint (OBJ-X) at 90 km inland. Blue Force holds area defense along the coastline and must prevent Red from reaching OBJ-X for 144 hours across six phases: PRE-H, PHASE 1, PHASE 2A, PHASE 2B, PHASE 3, and RESOLUTION.
purpose_ar: يُحاكي التمرين الحربي الثالث اقتحاماً برمائياً متنازعاً عليه في منطقة سواحل بريقة. يسعى الفريق الأحمر إلى الاستيلاء على منتصف خط أنابيب ناصر-بريقة (الهدف X) على عمق 90 كم في الداخل. يتولى الفريق الأزرق الدفاع عن المنطقة على طول الساحل ويجب عليه منع الفريق الأحمر من الوصول إلى الهدف X خلال 144 ساعة عبر ست مراحل.
end_state_en: Red secures OBJ-X and establishes a sustainable bridgehead inland. Blue prevents Red from reaching OBJ-X. Outcome determined in RESOLUTION phase.
end_state_ar: يؤمّن الفريق الأحمر الهدف X ويُرسّخ رأس جسر مستدام في العمق. يمنع الفريق الأزرق الفريق الأحمر من الوصول إلى الهدف X. تُحدّد مرحلة الحل النتيجة النهائية.
constraints: Blue force must not abandon the pipeline corridor
No offensive air operations beyond AO boundary
BLS sites must be contested before amphibious lodgement is declared
assumptions: Red amphibious force has naval fire superiority offshore
Blue logistics line remains intact through Phase 2A
EW environment is contested throughout all phases

actors.action_what: Initiate continuous EW operations to jam Blue's C2.
actors.action_why: Persistent EW will degrade Blue's ability to coordinate their defense and react to Red's operations.
actors.action_intended_effect: Reduce Blue's command and control effectiveness.

actors.action_what: Deploy mine layers to reinforce sea denial and prepare the approach corridor.
actors.action_why: Establishing sea control is essential for power projection and protecting the landing force.
actors.action_intended_effect: Secure the approach corridor by deterring Blue naval movements.

actors.action_what: Deploy mine sweepers to clear a path through Blue's minefields.
actors.action_why: Clearing mines is necessary to ensure safe passage for the amphibious landing forces.
actors.action_intended_effect: Create a secure corridor for landing operations.

actors.action_what: Deploy UAVs for ISR to identify Blue's defensive positions and reserve locations.
actors.action_why: ISR is crucial for identifying gaps and weaknesses in Blue's defenses to exploit during the assault.
actors.action_intended_effect: Gain actionable intelligence on Blue's defensive layout.
action_doctrine_cited: AJP-3.3 Air & Space Operations Ed B

actors.action_what: Conduct a UAV strike on identified Blue radar installations.
actors.action_why: Disrupting Blue's radar capabilities will degrade their situational awareness and defensive response.
actors.action_intended_effect: Reduce Blue's radar coverage and reaction time.

actors.action_what: Insert SOF to conduct reconnaissance and disrupt Blue's rear-area C2.
actors.action_why: Vertical envelopment with SOF can create chaos and confusion in Blue's command structure.
actors.action_intended_effect: Disrupt Blue's command and control in the rear area.

actors.action_what: Held in reserve — preserving SSM magazine for H-hour saturation salvo per opening-day concentration doctrine.
actors.action_why: Holding SSMs for a decisive initial strike aligns with the doctrine of overwhelming the defender's intercept capacity at the critical moment.
actors.action_intended_effect: Maximize impact during the initial assault by conserving strategic missiles.

actors.action_what: Held position — preserving strategic assets for future engagement per ADP 3-0.
actors.action_why: Strategic assets are held to ensure readiness for decisive engagements when Red reaches culmination.
actors.action_intended_effect: Maintain strategic advantage and readiness for future phases.

actors.action_what: Deploy ATGM battalion to cover minefields and engage Red mine sweepers.
actors.action_why: Ensure minefields are effective by providing overwatch and fire support.
actors.action_intended_effect: Prevent Red from clearing minefields and maintain sea denial.
action_doctrine_cited: Doctrines.md - Countermobility = terrain reinforcement + obstacle employment

actors.action_what: Deploy signal battalion to monitor and counter Red SOF reconnaissance activities.
actors.action_why: Protect rear-area C2 from disruption and maintain communication integrity.
actors.action_intended_effect: Deny Red SOF effective reconnaissance and disruption capabilities.
action_doctrine_cited: Doctrines.md - Defense types: area defense, mobile defense, retrograde

actors.action_what: Initiate counter-EW operations to protect Blue's C2 from Red jamming efforts.
actors.action_why: Ensure continuous communication and command capabilities.
actors.action_intended_effect: Neutralize Red's EW operations and maintain C2 effectiveness.
action_doctrine_cited: Doctrines.md - Air defense planning at brigade and below

actors.action_what: Deploy coastal support boats to monitor and disrupt Red mine-laying operations.
actors.action_why: Prevent Red from reinforcing sea denial and ensure our minefields remain effective.
actors.action_intended_effect: Disrupt Red's mine-laying efforts and maintain control over the maritime domain.
action_doctrine_cited: Doctrines.md - Obstacle effects: disrupt, fix, turn, block

actors.action_what: Activate coastal radar systems to detect and track incoming UAVs targeting radar installations.
actors.action_why: Protect critical radar infrastructure from UAV strikes.
actors.action_intended_effect: Preserve radar capabilities and maintain situational awareness.
action_doctrine_cited: Doctrines.md - Air defense planning at brigade and below

actors.action_what: Deploy F-16 squadron to intercept Red UAVs conducting ISR.
actors.action_why: Prevent Red from gaining intelligence on Blue defensive positions and reserves.
actors.action_intended_effect: Deny Red ISR capabilities and protect Blue's defensive posture.
